name: TensorFeed AI Gear
description: Curated, human-reviewed catalog of AI-relevant consumer hardware. Companion to https://tensorfeed.ai/gear with affiliate plumbing stripped.

products.name: Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 10 (16-inch, RTX 5090)
products.blurb: A 24GB VRAM RTX 5090 laptop that runs 30B parameter models locally in 4-bit without choking, and trains LoRA adapters on consumer datasets overnight.
products.description: Lenovo's flagship gaming and AI workstation pairs Intel's 24-core Core Ultra 9 275HX with a mobile RTX 5090 (24GB GDDR7, 175W TGP), a combo that punches well above most desktop tower CPUs. The 16-inch 2.5K OLED runs at 240Hz, which matters less for AI than the thermals: Lenovo's Coldfront cooling sustains the GPU at full power for long inference sessions.
specs: NVIDIA RTX 5090 Laptop GPU, 24GB GDDR7, up to 175W TGP
products.aiUseCase: Local LLMs up to 30B 4-bit quantized, Claude Code at full speed, Stable Diffusion XL, LoRA fine-tuning on 7B-13B models

products.name: ASUS ROG Strix SCAR 18 (2026) G835
products.blurb: An 18-inch mobile workstation with a 24GB RTX 5090 and up to 128GB of DDR5, which means you can hold a full 70B model in system RAM and stream layers to the GPU for hybrid inference.
products.description: The SCAR 18 leans heavier into the AI workstation pitch than any other Strix model. The standout is the 128GB max RAM ceiling and 8TB max storage, both user upgradeable, alongside the world's first 4K 240Hz Mini LED laptop panel. Vapor chamber cooling with liquid metal on both CPU and GPU lets the RTX 5090 hold its 175W TGP under sustained load, which is the actual gating factor on long training jobs.
specs: NVIDIA RTX 5090 Laptop GPU, 24GB GDDR7, 175W TGP
18-inch 4K Mini LED, 240Hz, 1600 nits peak, 2000+ dimming zones
products.aiUseCase: Local LLMs up to 30B fully on GPU, 70B with CPU offload, Stable Diffusion 3.5, multi-day fine-tuning runs with adequate thermals

products.name: Razer Blade 18 (2026)
products.blurb: Razer's flagship configures up to 128GB DDR5 alongside the 24GB RTX 5090, putting it in the same hybrid-inference tier as the SCAR 18 but in a sleeker CNC aluminum chassis.
products.description: The 2026 refresh moves the Blade 18 to Intel's Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus and offers GPUs from RTX 5070 Ti up to RTX 5090 (200W thermal budget). The 18-inch dual-mode display flips between UHD+ at 240Hz and FHD+ at 440Hz. You pay a steep premium over the SCAR for the build quality, the Thunderbolt 5 port, and the slimmest 18-inch chassis on the market.
specs: Intel Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus, 24 cores, 5.5 GHz boost
NVIDIA RTX 5090 Laptop GPU, 24GB GDDR7, 200W thermal budget
products.aiUseCase: Local LLMs 30B on GPU, 70B with CPU offload, Stable Diffusion workflows, on-the-road agent development

products.name: Apple MacBook Pro M5 Max (14-inch and 16-inch)
products.blurb: Up to 128GB of unified memory at 614 GB/s makes the M5 Max the most capable consumer laptop for fitting larger LLMs entirely in memory, and the silent thermals run for hours on battery.
products.description: The M5 Max ships with an 18-core CPU and 32-core GPU, with up to 128GB of unified memory addressable by both CPU and GPU. For agent developers, the win is that a 70B model in 4-bit (around 40GB) fits comfortably alongside Xcode, a browser, and a few Docker containers. MLX and llama.cpp Metal builds are mature; CUDA workflows are not an option.
specs: Apple M5 Max, 18-core CPU (6 efficiency + 12 performance)
32-core GPU with hardware ray tracing and Neural Engine
Up to 128GB unified memory, 614 GB/s bandwidth
14.2-inch or 16.2-inch Liquid Retina XDR, Wi-Fi 7, Thunderbolt 5
products.aiUseCase: Local LLMs up to 70B 4-bit via MLX or llama.cpp, on-device whisper transcription, MLX fine-tuning of 7B-13B models, Claude Code on battery for hours

products.name: Framework Laptop 16 with RTX 5070 Graphics Module
products.blurb: The only modular laptop where the GPU is a user-swappable cartridge, so when the RTX 6070 module ships you upgrade the laptop, not replace it.
products.description: Framework's 16-inch chassis pairs an AMD Ryzen AI 300 series APU with a removable NVIDIA RTX 5070 (8GB or 12GB GDDR7) graphics module that drops in via a Framework expansion bay. The 8GB module is the practical baseline for AI work; the 12GB module is the better fit for 13B class models. Repairability and upgrade path are the actual differentiators, not raw performance.
specs: AMD Ryzen AI 7 350 or Ryzen AI 9 HX 370
NVIDIA RTX 5070 Laptop GPU module, 8GB or 12GB GDDR7, 100W TGP on AC
512GB to 2TB NVMe SSD, modular expansion cards
products.aiUseCase: Local LLMs 7B-13B 4-bit, Stable Diffusion 1.5 and SDXL, light fine-tuning with LoRA, agent prototyping with a repairability-first hardware story

products.name: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090
products.blurb: 32GB of GDDR7 at 1,792 GB/s makes the 5090 the highest VRAM consumer GPU shipping, fitting a full 30B model in fp8 or a quantized 70B with room for context.
products.description: The Blackwell flagship pushes 21,760 CUDA cores and 170 SMs against a 575W TDP, delivering roughly 27 to 35 percent faster gaming than the 4090 and a similar lift on AI inference. The 32GB VRAM ceiling is the load-bearing spec for local LLM work: it crosses the 30B-at-fp8 threshold that the 4090 could not. Availability remains tight in May 2026 and street prices are well above the $1,999 MSRP.
specs: 5th gen Tensor cores, 4th gen RT cores, DLSS 4
PCIe 5.0 x16, three DisplayPort 2.1, one HDMI 2.1b
products.aiUseCase: Local LLMs up to 70B 4-bit quantized, 30B fp8, Stable Diffusion 3.5 and Flux at full resolution, single-GPU fine-tuning of 13B models

products.name: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080
products.blurb: 16GB of GDDR7 at 960 GB/s puts the 5080 right at the 13B-fp16 and 30B-4bit sweet spot for builders who want CUDA without the 5090 tax.
products.description: The 5080 ships 10,752 CUDA cores against a 360W TDP and pairs them with 16GB of GDDR7 on a 256-bit bus. For AI workloads, the practical ceiling is 13B class models in fp16 or 30B models in 4-bit quantization. It is the better value pick if VRAM is the bottleneck and you do not need to touch 70B class work locally.
specs: 5th gen Tensor cores, 4th gen RT cores
PCIe 5.0 x16, three DisplayPort 2.1, one HDMI 2.1b
products.aiUseCase: Local LLMs 13B fp16 or 30B 4-bit quantized, Stable Diffusion XL and 3.5, LoRA fine-tuning on 7B models, Claude Code at desktop speeds

products.name: NVIDIA RTX 6000 Ada Generation
products.blurb: 48GB of ECC GDDR6 in a 300W blower card is the right answer for builders who need a quiet workstation that can hold a 70B 4-bit model entirely on one GPU.
products.description: The RTX 6000 Ada is built on the previous Ada Lovelace architecture (not Blackwell), but the 48GB ECC VRAM and 300W blower form factor are exactly what dual-GPU and rack-mount workstations need. Two of these get you to 96GB of GPU memory in a desktop chassis without exotic cooling. The cost premium over a 5090 is justified mainly by the VRAM ceiling and ECC.
specs: 18,176 CUDA cores, 142 RT cores, 568 Tensor cores
PCIe 4.0 x16, four DisplayPort 1.4a, AV1 encode and decode
products.aiUseCase: Local LLMs up to 70B 4-bit on a single card, 13B fp16, multi-GPU fine-tuning at 96GB and beyond, professional rendering and simulation alongside AI

products.name: Meta Ray-Ban Display
products.blurb: The first mass-market AI glasses with an actual in-lens display, paired with an EMG wristband that reads finger twitches as input, making it the closest thing to a wearable agent UI in 2026.
products.description: Meta's Display generation adds a 600 by 600 monocular in-lens screen and the Meta Neural Band, an EMG wristband that picks up muscle signals from subtle finger movements and translates them into commands. The display sits at 42 pixels per degree across a 20-degree FOV with a 5,000 nit ceiling, which is bright enough to read in direct sun. Sold as a $799 bundle including the Neural Band.
specs: 600 x 600 monocular in-lens display, 90Hz refresh, 20-degree FOV
30 to 5,000 nits brightness, less than 2 percent light leakage
Qualcomm Snapdragon AR1 Gen 1, 12MP camera, 5 mics, dual off-ear speakers
Up to 6 hours mixed use, 30 hours with the case
Meta Neural Band EMG wristband included
products.aiUseCase: Conversational Meta AI agent on the lens, real-time translation, message and notification glanceables, first-person camera for vision-LLM workflows

products.name: Xreal One Pro
products.blurb: A 171-inch virtual 1080p display strapped to your face at 3.1 ounces, ideal for running a Claude Code session on a Steam Deck or laptop while traveling.
products.description: The One Pro uses Sony 0.55-inch Micro OLED panels behind X-Prism optics to project a 171-inch virtual display at 120Hz with a 57-degree field of view. The onboard X1 chip handles 3DoF spatial tracking so the screen can either anchor in space or follow your head. USB-C in, no phone required, no battery in the glasses.
specs: X1 chip for 3DoF spatial tracking, Real 3D 2D-to-3D conversion
Bose-tuned open-ear speakers, USB-C wired connection
products.aiUseCase: Portable second screen for Claude Code, agent development, or LLM chat workflows when paired with a Mac, Steam Deck, ROG Ally, or compatible phone

products.name: Even Realities G1
products.blurb: The lightest in-lens display glasses that pass for normal eyewear, designed around teleprompt, translate, navigate, and AI assistant flows rather than gaming or media.
products.description: Even Realities builds the G1 around a magnesium and titanium frame with tiny optical projectors hidden in the lens edge. There is no camera on the standard model, which makes them less polarizing in public than the Ray-Ban Display, and the daily wear story is genuinely closer to regular glasses. The trade-off is a more limited display and a heavier dependence on the phone app.
specs: Dual in-lens micro-projectors, monochrome green text overlay
Panto or rectangular frame, magnesium fused with sandstone
Hypoallergenic titanium and silicone temple tips
iOS and Android compatible, ambient brightness sensors
products.aiUseCase: Heads-up AI assistant prompts, live translation overlays, teleprompt for content creators, navigation, low-key wearable agent UI for daily wear

products.name: Brilliant Labs Frame
products.blurb: Open source AI glasses with full design files on GitHub, a microOLED, a camera, and Noa, a hackable LLM assistant that turns the frame into a dev kit rather than a finished product.
products.description: Brilliant Labs ships the Frame as a near-40g pair of glasses with a microOLED bonded to a prism in the right lens (640 by 400, 500 nits, 20-degree diagonal FOV), a camera over the nose bridge, and a microphone. The Nordic nRF52840 plus a Lattice CrosslinkNX FPGA handle on-device compute. The pitch is that everything is open source, so you can build your own agent on top of the included Noa assistant.
specs: Nordic nRF52840 ARM Cortex-M4F, Lattice CrosslinkNX FPGA
Camera on the brim, microphone, IPD 58 to 72mm
Under 40g, all-day battery with Mister Power charger
Fully open source hardware and software (design files on GitHub)
products.aiUseCase: Hackable wearable LLM dev kit, custom on-device vision models, Noa AI assistant out of the box, ideal for agent builders who want to own the stack

products.name: Plaud Note Pro
products.blurb: A 0.12-inch thin AI voice recorder that lives on the back of your phone and turns every meeting into a searchable, summarized transcript.
products.description: The Note Pro is the upgrade to Plaud's original card recorder. It uses four MEMS mics plus a voice processing unit to capture conversations at up to 16 feet, then pipes audio to the Plaud app for transcription in 112 languages with auto speaker labels and template-driven summaries.
specs: 4 MEMS mics + 1 VPU, captures voices up to 16.4 ft
products.aiUseCase: Off-device meeting capture, then on-cloud LLM transcription, multidimensional summaries, mind maps, and template-driven action items

products.name: Bee Pioneer
products.blurb: A $49 always-on AI wristband that listens to your day, then writes the to-do list, recap, and reminders for you.
products.description: The Pioneer looks like a Fitbit but acts as a personal AI assistant, with always-on mics that analyze ambient conversations and feed summaries, action items, and reminders into the Bee app. Amazon acquired the company in 2025, so the device sits at the low end of the AI-wearable price ladder while attached to a real distribution path.
specs: 7-day battery life, 160+ hours of always-on capture
Bluetooth pairing to iOS or Android
AI summaries, action items, and conversational memory
products.aiUseCase: Ambient life-logging plus auto-generated to-do lists, reminders, and daily recaps. Lowest barrier to entry for trying always-on AI wearables.

products.name: Friend
products.blurb: A $129 AI companion necklace that listens around the clock and texts you commentary like a slightly judgmental roommate.
products.description: Friend is Avi Schiffmann's controversial pendant, the spiritual continuation of his earlier Tab project. It hangs around your neck on a lanyard, listens continuously to ambient audio, and pushes opinionated AI-generated text messages back to your phone. There is no productivity layer, no summary view, no transcripts. The pitch is parasocial company, and it is divisive on purpose.
specs: Bluetooth pairing to a paired phone
products.aiUseCase: AI companionship and ambient social texting. A pure experiment in always-listening hardware, useful as a category marker more than a productivity tool.

products.name: Unitree Go2
products.blurb: The most accessible quadruped robot money can buy, with onboard LiDAR and a real SDK path for serious robotics work.
products.description: The Go2 lineup runs from a $1,600 Air variant up to $16K+ EDU models that ship with full SDK and ROS 2 access. The Pro sits in the middle at around $2,800 and is the realistic consumer-on-ramp. All variants run Unitree's L2 4D LiDAR with a 360 by 96 degree field of view and reach speeds up to 13 km/h.
specs: L2 4D LiDAR, 360 by 96 degree hemispherical FOV
EDU tiers unlock SDK, ROS 2, low-level motor control
products.aiUseCase: Custom robotics research, autonomy experiments, LiDAR-based mapping, and the closest you can get to a hobbyist humanoid platform without spending Boston Dynamics money.

products.name: Unitree G1
products.blurb: A 127 cm humanoid robot with 23 degrees of freedom, available off the shelf for $16K.
products.description: The G1 is the lowest barrier to entry for a fully bipedal humanoid robot in 2026. The base configuration ships with 23 DoF, 3D LiDAR, depth cameras, and force-controlled hands. EDU configurations open up SDK access and push the DoF count to 43 for researchers and developers who need full programmability.
specs: 23 to 43 degrees of freedom across configurations
Approximately 2 hours runtime under active use
products.aiUseCase: Hands-on humanoid robotics development, embodied AI research, motion-policy experimentation, and the most affordable platform for ML researchers who want to deploy on a real bipedal robot.

products.name: 1X NEO
products.blurb: A 66-pound household humanoid robot built for the home, now taking preorders with first deliveries to US homes in 2026.
products.description: NEO is the first humanoid pitched explicitly at consumer homes, not factories or research labs. It runs on a 3D lattice polymer soft body, has 22 DoF hands, and operates at a refrigerator-quiet 22 dB. First deliveries are slated for US homes in 2026, with international expansion in 2027.
specs: 66 lb weight, lifts 150 lb, carries 55 lb
products.aiUseCase: Whole-home household automation: fetching items, opening doors, light operation. First commercial test of a household-class general-purpose humanoid.

products.name: NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano Super Developer Kit
products.blurb: NVIDIA cut the Orin Nano dev kit to $249 and bumped its AI throughput in the same move, making it the runaway leader in hobbyist edge AI.
products.description: The Super refresh keeps the same hardware as the original Orin Nano dev kit but unlocks higher clocks and a new power profile, pushing AI compute up to roughly 67 TOPS. At $249 it is the cheapest practical platform for running real small-LM and computer-vision workloads locally on a single board.
specs: Up to ~67 TOPS sparse INT8 AI performance
M.2 Key M and Key E slots
products.aiUseCase: Local LLM inference at the small-model tier, robotics dev kits, vision pipelines, and the standard reference platform for the entire LeRobot and Isaac ROS stack.

products.name: Hailo-8 M.2 AI Accelerator
products.blurb: A 26 TOPS M.2 card that drops into a Raspberry Pi 5 or mini PC and quietly outclasses every Edge TPU on the market.
products.description: The Hailo-8 M.2 module is the de facto upgrade path now that the Raspberry Pi AI Kit is out of production. It delivers 26 TOPS at roughly 2.5 W in an M.2 form factor with support for TensorFlow, ONNX, Keras, and PyTorch. Pairs cleanly with the Pi 5 via PCIe.
specs: PCIe Gen 3 x2 or x4 depending on key
TensorFlow, TensorFlow Lite, ONNX, Keras, PyTorch
products.aiUseCase: Edge vision pipelines, real-time object detection, NVR-style smart cameras, and any embedded build that needs more headroom than a Coral TPU but less heat than a Jetson.

products.name: Rabbit R1
products.blurb: The original orange AI handheld, still shipping in 2026 with a roadmap that now includes a CLI-flavored cyberdeck successor.
products.description: The R1 was the most-mocked AI device of 2024 and somehow outlived nearly all the wearables that ridiculed it. Rabbit raised fresh funding, kept shipping LAM updates, and is now actively developing a follow-up cyberdeck form factor focused on agent workflows and a real command-line surface.
specs: Rabbit OS with LAM agent layer
products.aiUseCase: Standalone always-with-you agent device, useful for testing agentic UX patterns without pulling out a phone. Mostly interesting as a category landmark and live experiment.

products.name: DJI Osmo Pocket 3
products.blurb: A 1-inch sensor gimbal camera whose ActiveTrack 6.0 subject tracking is one of the best on-device computer vision implementations shipping today.
products.description: The Pocket 3 is the de facto pocket vlog camera, but the AI angle is its tracking stack: ActiveTrack 6.0 keeps human subjects centered through occlusion and re-acquisition, Face Auto-Detect locks faces automatically, and Dynamic Framing handles reframing for vertical and horizontal targets in real time.
products.aiUseCase: Hands-free vlogging with on-device subject tracking, single-operator interview capture, AI-assisted reframing for short-form video pipelines.

products.name: Insta360 X5
products.blurb: A 360-degree 8K action cam whose AI app auto-edits highlight reels and runs low-light denoising as a post-capture pipeline.
products.description: The X5 captures 8K 360 footage with replaceable lenses, then leans on Insta360's app-side AI to do the heavy lifting: highlight detection, automatic music-synced cuts, low-light reconstruction, and AI-driven reframing of the spherical capture into traditional 16:9 or vertical output.
products.aiUseCase: Capture-once-edit-later content workflows, automatic highlight reel generation, AI reframing from 360 source to flat output.

products.name: Amazon Echo Show 11
products.blurb: The first Echo built around Alexa+, with a custom AZ3 Pro chip running on-device language models and vision transformers.
products.description: The Echo Show 11 is Amazon's flagship Alexa+ device, built on the AZ3 Pro silicon with native support for language models and vision transformers. The Omnisense sensor platform fuses a 13 MP camera, ultrasound, Wi-Fi radar, accelerometer, and Wi-Fi CSI into a single ambient AI loop. Alexa+ ships out of the box on new orders.
specs: 13 MP camera plus Omnisense ambient sensors
products.aiUseCase: Voice-first home hub, ambient-AI presence detection, multi-step household agent commands, the canonical consumer test case for an agent that lives in the kitchen.

products.name: Google Nest Cam (3rd gen) with Gemini for Home
products.blurb: 2K HDR Nest Cams that hand video to Gemini so you can ask what happened to the vase in the living room and get a real answer.
products.description: The third-generation Nest Cam Indoor and second-generation Outdoor pair 2K HDR capture with Gemini for Home, Google's multimodal AI layer for the smart home. Detailed alerts, video search, and natural-language summaries replace the old motion-zone shuffle.
products.aiUseCase: Natural-language video search, AI-generated event summaries, detailed multimodal alerts. One of the first mainstream consumer products where you actually talk to your security footage.

products.name: Shure MV7i Smart Microphone
products.blurb: A USB and XLR podcast mic with a built-in audio interface and on-board AI DSP that handles gain, denoise, and gating automatically.
products.description: The MV7i fuses microphone, interface, and AI processing into a single chassis. Auto Level Mode with SmartGate dynamically adjusts gain based on proximity, speech volume, and room acoustics. The DSP runs a real-time denoiser plus Voice Isolation Technology that separates speech from ambient noise.
products.aiUseCase: AI podcasting and live streaming setups where automatic gain riding, noise removal, and voice isolation replace a human operator. Strong fit for solo creators and remote-recording workflows.

products.name: Elgato Stream Deck Plus
products.blurb: With Stream Deck 7.4, the LCD-key controller now speaks MCP, which means Claude or ChatGPT can fire any key as a tool call.
products.description: The Stream Deck Plus combines eight LCD keys, four dials, and a touch strip. Stream Deck 7.4 added Model Context Protocol support, which surfaces any configured action as an MCP tool that Claude, ChatGPT, or NVIDIA G-Assist can call. The MCP Actions profile becomes a dedicated agent control surface.
specs: Stream Deck 7.4 software with MCP support
products.aiUseCase: Building a tactile agent control panel where Claude or other LLM agents can trigger OBS scenes, smart-home routines, AI workflows, or app shortcuts via MCP calls.

products.name: Comma 3X
products.blurb: A $1,249 windshield-mount box that runs openpilot and adds Tesla-grade lane-keeping plus longitudinal assist to 325+ stock vehicles.
products.description: The Comma 3X is the third-gen openpilot device, with a triple-camera array and lower price tag than its predecessor. It plugs into a compatible car's harness and adds end-to-end driver assistance trained on Comma's fleet data. It is the only consumer-purchasable, fully open-source driver-assist hardware in production.
products.aiUseCase: Aftermarket lane centering plus adaptive cruise on a stock car, driving an open-source ADAS stack you can audit and retrain. The most accessible self-driving R and D platform on the road.

products.name: AI Engineering by Chip Huyen
products.blurb: The most-read book on the O'Reilly platform in 2025, framing the entire LLM application stack from foundation model choice through deployment.
products.description: Chip Huyen's followup to Designing Machine Learning Systems covers the practical engineering of LLM and multimodal-model applications: model selection, prompt engineering, evaluation, RAG, agents, finetuning, inference optimization, and observability. It is the standard reference for the foundation-model era of ML engineering.
products.aiUseCase: The canonical text for AI engineers shipping production LLM applications. Pairs with Huyen's earlier Designing Machine Learning Systems for the full ML-systems curriculum.

products.name: Designing Machine Learning Systems by Chip Huyen
products.blurb: The book that taught a generation of ML engineers how to ship real systems before LLMs reset the conversation.
products.description: Chip Huyen's 2022 O'Reilly title remains the canonical reference for the non-LLM ML stack: data pipelines, feature engineering, training and serving infrastructure, monitoring, and the production loop. Translated into more than ten languages and used in Stanford's CS 329S curriculum.
products.aiUseCase: Foundational reading for anyone building classical ML systems alongside LLMs, especially data engineers, ML platform engineers, and engineering managers.

products.name: The Coming Wave by Mustafa Suleyman
products.blurb: The DeepMind cofounder's case for why containment, not capability, is the defining problem of the AI era.
products.description: Written with Michael Bhaskar, the book argues that AI and synthetic biology together create a containment problem unlike anything previous technology has produced. Suleyman, now CEO of Microsoft AI, draws on his DeepMind and Inflection AI experience to frame the political and economic stakes. Named a best book of the year by CNN, The Economist, Bloomberg, and the Financial Times.
products.aiUseCase: Required context for anyone working on AI policy, alignment, or governance, and a clean argument primer for talking to non-technical audiences about why this matters.

products.name: Crucial Pro 64GB DDR5-6400 Kit
products.blurb: A 2x32GB DDR5-6400 kit sized exactly for running 70B-class local models with headroom for the rest of your machine.
products.description: Modern local LLM workflows need real RAM, not just VRAM. A 70B model in 4-bit quantization wants 64 GB or more of system memory once you account for context window, KV cache, and the rest of the OS. This Crucial Pro kit is the cheapest path to that target with XMP 3.0 and EXPO overclocking support.
specs: Intel XMP 3.0 and AMD EXPO profiles
products.aiUseCase: Running 70B-class quantized LLMs locally with llama.cpp, Ollama, or LM Studio, plus heavy multi-app workflows around an AI dev environment.

products.name: WD_BLACK SN850X 4TB
products.blurb: A 4 TB PCIe Gen4 NVMe SSD with the speed budget to host a full quantized 70B model plus your working datasets.
products.description: The SN850X 4TB is the sweet spot for local AI builds. At 7,300 MB/s sequential read it loads model weights fast enough to keep cold-start times reasonable, and 4 TB of capacity holds multiple quantized models, datasets, and embedding stores without the constant juggling of a 1 TB drive.
products.aiUseCase: Local LLM weight storage, dataset caching, embedding stores, and the obvious upgrade for any workstation that runs llama.cpp, ComfyUI, or training jobs locally.
